headings.text: Prevent Injection Molding Defects and Causes
Stop Over-Engineering: Converting Metal Parts to Plastic
Know Your Materials: Acrylonitrile Styrene Acrylate (ASA)

meta_description: Here are a few of the most common defects that may occur in plastic injection molding and how product teams can avoid them.

sections.heading: 7‚Äå ‚ÄåCommon‚Äå ‚ÄåInjection‚Äå ‚ÄåMolding‚Äå ‚ÄåDefects‚Äå ‚Äåand‚Äå ‚ÄåHow‚Äå ‚Äåto‚Äå ‚ÄåAvoid‚Äå ‚ÄåThem‚Äå
sections.text: 7‚Äå ‚ÄåCommon‚Äå ‚ÄåInjection‚Äå ‚ÄåMolding‚Äå ‚ÄåDefects‚Äå ‚Äåand‚Äå ‚ÄåHow‚Äå ‚Äåto‚Äå ‚ÄåAvoid‚Äå ‚ÄåThem‚Äå Published on January 18, 2024 Injection molding is an efficient production method when high volumes of identical parts are needed. However, it also requires a high degree of technical expertise to master. With so many different variables in play, small, seemingly minor mistakes in early phases of product development can lead to major problems ‚Äî and even compromise product integrity ‚Äî down the line. Defects can reduce the speed and cost-efficiency of the entire product development process, and can potentially shorten product life spans if left unchecked. Injection molding issues and defects can be caused by a host of...
